pr.title: Rename ftKb_SpecialNFx to ftkirbyspecialfox, match most functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed Kirby's copied Fox/Falco neutral-special source unit from ftKb_SpecialNFx.c to ftkirbyspecialfox.c, updated GALE01 splits/configure/symbols, gave two anonymous fn_ symbols purpose names, typed the Kirby fighter-var blaster pointer, and added C implementations for most of the copied blaster start/loop/end logic. decomp.dev reported matched code at 55.24% (+0.04%, +1592 bytes), with 36 new matches under the new unit and two remaining near-matches for ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100/800FE240.
key_files: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox.c — new renamed source with expanded Kirby Fox/Falco copied-neutral-special implementation
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/types.h — changed fv.kb.xB0 from u32 to HSD_GObj* with Fox/Falco blaster item comment
config/GALE01/splits.txt — split path changed to ftkirbyspecialfox.c for 0x800FDC70-0x800FEDD0
config/GALE01/symbols.txt — symbol names updated for 0x800FDCE0 and 0x800FE0E0
configure.py — NonMatching object path updated to the renamed source file
what_changed: Replaced the old 372-line ftKb_SpecialNFx.c source path with a 713-line ftkirbyspecialfox.c file and updated both splits.txt and configure.py to compile the renamed object.
Renamed fn_800FDCE0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_OnChangeAction and fn_800FE0E0 to ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot in both symbols.txt and ftKb_Init.h.
Added matched C for ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDC70, including a right-hand joint offset lookup via lb_8000B1CC and a zero-vector fallback when the gobj/fighter is null.

Reworked motion-state handling for Fox and Falco copied blaster states using ftKb_MS_* and ftCo_MS_* enum names instead of raw case constants in functions such as ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDD4C and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDDF4.
Added inline helpers for shared Fox/Falco behavior: ftKb_SpecialNFx_SetCall, ftKbGetBlasterId, and ground/air start/loop/end motion-id selectors.

Implemented much of the blaster item setup and animation flow: it_802AE8A8 blaster creation, item action updates, loop-to-loop and loop-to-end motion transitions, accessory4_cb assignment, callback setup, cleanup, and air freefall/fall-special handoff.
Moved existing IASA, Phys, Coll, and later Ness flash helper functions into the renamed file while preserving address-order content.
Changed ftKb_FighterVars.xB0 from u32 to HSD_GObj* and documented it as the Fox/Falco blaster item.
smart_moves: Updated all object-path consumers together: source filename, GALE01 split entry, and configure.py object list. This avoids build/split drift during a source-unit rename.
Kept externally visible function prefixes as ftKb_SpecialNFx_* and ftKb_FxSpecial* while using the clearer lowercase source filename ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
Converted the blaster item slot from an integer-like u32 to HSD_GObj*, which made item-object ownership clearer and removed the need to treat the field as an opaque scalar.
Used small inline helper functions to centralize Fox-vs-Falco differences in item id and motion id selection without creating extra exported symbols.
Retained match-sensitive code-shape devices visible in the diff, including FORCE_PAD_STACK_8, PAD_STACK, volatile padding, temporary HSD_GObj variables, and nested variable declarations.
decomp_lessons: When renaming a decomp source unit, update splits.txt and configure.py in the same PR; decomp.dev may report old-unit broken matches and new-unit matches as rename accounting noise.

Replacing raw motion-id case labels with enum names is safe once the enum values are known, but this PR still preserved offset arithmetic such as motion_id - 0x1A7, motion_id - 0x208, and motion_id - 0xD6 for the returned index mapping.
Struct-field typing can be a matching aid and a readability win: xB0's HSD_GObj* type makes the blaster item lifecycle easier to track across creation, callbacks, item updates, and cleanup.

For fighter action code, callback ordering matters: the new code repeatedly resets death/take-damage callbacks via ftKb_SpecialNFx_SetCall and assigns accessory4_cb around Fighter_ChangeMotionState transitions.

Not all newly decompiled-looking code was fully matched: the bot still listed ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 at 98.62% and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 at 98.75%, and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30 remained represented by a /// # marker in the diff.
naming_conventions: Source filename changed from mixed-case ftKb_SpecialNFx.c to lowercase ftkirbyspecialfox.c.
Function symbols retained the ftKb_SpecialNFx_* prefix for Kirby's copied Fox/Falco neutral-special helpers.
Action callbacks use the ftKb_FxSpecialN*/ftKb_FxSpecialAirN* pattern with Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll suffixes.
Anonymous fn_ symbols were replaced with purpose names: ftKb_SpecialNFx_OnChangeAction and ftKb_SpecialNFx_CreateBlasterShot.
The field xB0 was not renamed, but its type/comment now identify it as the Fox/Falco blaster item.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 and PAD_STACK(8) or PAD_STACK(4 * 4) to preserve stack layout in small helper and cleanup/init functions.
Kept explicit padding locals such as volatile int pad and unsigned long long pad in animation code.
Used inline helper functions for repeated Fox/Falco selection logic, likely avoiding extra symbols while keeping the generated code shape manageable.
Preserved match-sensitive temporary variables and assignments such as HSD_GObj* temp and temp = gobj inside Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls.
Maintained exact command-var update order and item-call ordering around it_802ADDD0, it_802AE538, it_802AE608, and ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining checks.
Used enum case labels for readability while retaining magic subtraction constants for computed special-N indices.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice, and the PR body was null.
decomp-dev bot reported 36 new matches under main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialfox and 30 broken matches under the deleted ftKb_SpecialNFx unit, consistent with the file rename.
The same bot report listed 3 improvements in unmatched items: ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100, ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240, and .sdata2.
follow_up_queries: Can ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE100 and ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FE240 be brought from 98.x% to 100% match?
What remains for ftKb_SpecialNFx_800FDF30, which still appeared as a /// # placeholder in this diff?
Should fv.kb.xB0 eventually be renamed from its offset-style name now that it is documented as a Fox/Falco blaster HSD_GObj*?
Are there other Kirby copied-special source files that should follow the lowercase ftkirbyspecial<character>.c filename convention?
Can decomp.dev/file-rename accounting be normalized so old-unit broken matches are not confused with true regressions?
